steps.title: Identify the zeros on the graph
steps.content: A zero of $f(x)$ occurs where the curve crosses the $x$-axis, meaning $y = 0$. Examining the complete graph, the black curve intersects the $x$-axis at exactly one point, located near $x = 4$.

steps.title: Count the x-intercepts
steps.content: Scan the entire visible domain from left to right. The curve crosses the $x$-axis once. There are no other points where the graph touches or crosses the horizontal axis. That matches Choice B — One.
